warnings: Exhibit C (Financial Statements) page is present but the actual financial statement figures are not rendered in the extracted text — only the cover page is visible; financial condition fields populated from what is disclosed in Item 21 narrative only.

Franchisor entity was formed March 31, 2026 — only an audited opening balance sheet (April 14, 2026) and predecessor audited statements are available; no multi-year income statement data for the franchisor itself.
Only 2 franchised outlets exist as of December 31, 2025; Item 19 data is based on company-owned/affiliate locations only, limiting comparability to franchisee performance.

extracted.brandBackground: Story Time Chess is a children's chess and games education brand founded through affiliate Thinking Cup Games LLC (est. 2017), operating since 2011 in Hong Kong and offering U.S. franchises since May 2025.
extracted.conceptRationale: The franchise delivers chess and games instruction to children ages 3–8 at schools, community venues, and homes — a children's educational enrichment service.
extracted.staffingRationale: Franchisee operates from a home-based office, travels to venues to teach, and may hire instructors; Item 15 requires owner/manager full-time involvement but no brick-and-mortar staff.

leadership.name: Paul Levy
leadership.background: CEO of Story Time Chess Franchising LLC and parent Thinking Cup Inc. since April 2024; prior COO/co-founder of Burst.it Inc. for 12 years.
leadership.whyItMatters: Top executive responsible for overall franchise strategy and operations.

leadership.name: Jon Sieber
leadership.background: Co-founder of Thinking Cup Learning since 2015 and President of franchising since inception; helped write the chess curriculum and trained hundreds of tutors.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads franchise development and conducts initial training.

leadership.name: Harlan J. Alford
leadership.role: Co-Founder and President of Owned and Operated Units
leadership.background: Co-founder of Thinking Cup Learning since 2015; oversees company-owned locations and curriculum tutoring.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees the company-owned unit operations that generate the Item 19 data.

leadership.name: Edward Huang
leadership.background: General counsel and technology product manager at Thinking Cup Inc. since January 2023; prior founder/COO of Seed & Stone Inc.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages curriculum IP and technology systems franchisees must use.

leadership.name: Jessica Finney
leadership.background: VP of Curriculum & Training at Thinking Cup Inc. since January 2024; built the mentorship and tutor training program over 10+ years.
leadership.whyItMatters: Designs and delivers the franchisee training program.

leadership.name: Nicholas Nieves
leadership.role: Senior Director of Growth and Tutor Operations
leadership.background: Senior Director at Thinking Cup Inc. since January 2024; previously Tutor Director at Chess at Three.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages tutor operations and growth initiatives supporting franchisees.

item19.notes: Item 19 discloses data for one company-owned affiliate location in Los Angeles, CA (representative of 2 territories, open since 2022). A separate gross-sales breakdown for two New York company-owned locations (without retail) is also disclosed. No franchisee financial performance data is included. The LA P&L includes a pro-forma adjustment for franchise-related expenses not incurred by the company-owned outlet. Data is unaudited.

cohorts.label: LA Company-Owned Location – Gross Sales
cohorts.basis: 2025 full-year gross sales for the Los Angeles, CA company-owned affiliate location (representative of 2 territories); unaudited.

cohorts.label: LA Company-Owned Location – Net Profit Less Disclosed Expenses
cohorts.basis: 2025 gross profit less disclosed expenses (direct labor, legal/professional, merchant processing, advertising, software, insurance) for LA location; excludes royalty, brand fund, and technology fees.

cohorts.label: LA Company-Owned Location – Net Profit Less Disclosed and Franchise Expenses
cohorts.basis: 2025 pro-forma net profit after adding estimated franchise-related expenses (7% royalty, 2% brand fund, 1% technology fee) not incurred by the company-owned outlet; unaudited.

cohorts.label: New York Company-Owned Locations (UWS + Tribeca) – Gross Sales
cohorts.basis: 2025 combined gross sales for two NY company-owned locations (UWS: $385,001; Tribeca: $303,264), excluding retail offerings; comingled bookkeeping prevents unit-level P&L.

lineItems.notes: Lump sum due on signing; non-refundable. Item 5, p.10
Branded signage, printing, and equipment for approximately 3 months. Item 7, p.16
Board games, lesson books, and marketing materials (backpacks, flyers); paid to franchisor. Item 7, p.16

lineItems.category: Computer System and Point of Sale System
lineItems.notes: Hardware and software meeting minimum specs; includes desktop, iPad, and initial software license. Item 7, p.16
Local business licenses; cost varies by location. Item 7, p.17
Attorney and accountant fees for evaluating the offering and entity formation. Item 7, p.17
Quarterly premium estimate; multiple coverage types required. Item 7, p.17
Out-of-pocket travel, lodging, meals for up to 2 trainees attending initial training. Item 7, p.17
Minimum required opening advertising spend. Item 7, p.17
Estimated working capital for first 3 months; excludes payroll, royalties, and brand fund fees. Item 7, p.17

hiddenCosts.name: Tutor Cruncher Business Management System
hiddenCosts.description: Required ongoing subscription to the designated POS/business management system; estimated $360–$960 per year.

hiddenCosts.name: Other Required Software and Applications
hiddenCosts.description: Additional required third-party software subscriptions beyond the POS system; estimated $30–$80 per month.

hiddenCosts.name: Annual Conference / Business Meeting Registration
hiddenCosts.description: Up to $500 per person registration fee for mandatory annual business meeting (up to 3 days), plus travel and lodging at franchisee's expense.

hiddenCosts.name: Additional Training Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $500 per trainer per day for remedial or additional in-territory training, plus trainer travel expenses.

hiddenCosts.name: Background Check Fees
hiddenCosts.description: Some states require mandatory background checks for individuals providing instructional services to children; fees paid to state agencies.

hiddenCosts.name: Credit Card Processing Fees (Stripe)
hiddenCosts.description: Percentage-based fees on all credit card processed gross revenue through the required Stripe integration.

hiddenCosts.name: Operations Standard Violation Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $250–$750 per occurrence for failure to comply with contractual or operations manual requirements.

hiddenCosts.name: Liquidated Damages on Termination
hiddenCosts.description: Greater of $50,000 or average monthly royalty and brand fund fees multiplied by up to 24 months remaining in term, due upon franchisee-default termination.

hiddenCosts.name: Interim Management Support Fee
hiddenCosts.description: 20% of gross revenue plus costs if franchisor provides on-site or virtual management of the franchised business.

hiddenCosts.name: Transfer Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $10,000 flat fee payable upon submission of a transfer application.

hiddenCosts.name: Successor Agreement Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $10,000 fee due before signing a renewal/successor franchise agreement.

hiddenCosts.name: Advertising Cooperative Contribution
hiddenCosts.description: If formed, up to 2% of gross revenue per month; creditable up to 50% against local advertising requirement.

operationalRisks.title: Unregistered Primary Trademark
operationalRisks.description: The primary logo/design mark used in the business is not federally registered; a successful challenge could require rebranding at franchisee's expense.

operationalRisks.title: Very Early-Stage Franchise System
operationalRisks.description: Only 2 franchised outlets existed as of December 31, 2025, and the franchisor entity was formed March 31, 2026 — extremely limited franchisee track record.

operationalRisks.title: Supplier Control / Captive Purchasing
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee must purchase nearly all supplies and classroom materials exclusively from the franchisor or designated suppliers at prices set by the franchisor, which may exceed market rates.

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Liability Guarantee
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee's spouse must personally guarantee all financial obligations under the franchise agreement, putting marital assets at risk.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: All disputes must be resolved by mediation, arbitration, or litigation in Tennessee, which may disadvantage franchisees located in other states.

operationalRisks.title: Non-Exclusive Territory
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee receives no exclusive territory; franchisor reserves rights to sell through alternate distribution channels, commercial accounts, and internet within the territory without compensation to franchisee.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Background Checks and Child Safety Compliance
operationalRisks.description: State laws may require background checks for all instructors working with children, adding regulatory compliance costs and potential hiring delays.

operationalRisks.title: Liquidated Damages on Default Termination
operationalRisks.description: Upon termination for franchisee default, franchisee owes the greater of $50,000 or up to 24 months of average royalty and brand fund fees as liquidated damages.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor May Change Business Model Without Consent
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor may modify the operations manual, system standards, and marks at any time, requiring franchisee to implement changes at franchisee's own cost.

operationalRisks.title: Territory May Be Reduced at Renewal
operationalRisks.description: At renewal, franchisor may re-evaluate and reduce the franchisee's territory based on population shifts, potentially shrinking the franchisee's market.

rentDetail.source: Item 7, p.16-17 — no lease/rent line item; franchisee operates from a home-based office with no required commercial lease.

riskReasons: 4 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.
notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $76,403 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $34,848/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: The franchise delivers chess and games instruction to children ages 3–8 at schools, community venues, and homes — a children's educational enrichment service.

benchmark.label: Education / childcare
benchmark.dominantRisk: Regulated staff ratios and enrollment/utilization. Labor dominates and ratios cap how lean you can run.
considerations: Labor is the dominant line at ~40–55% and is floored by regulated student/staff ratios.
Validate enrollment ramp and capacity-utilization curves with Item 20 operators.
Confirm licensing, facility, and compliance costs specific to your state — not in the FDD.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–20% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

insights.staffingNote: Flagged as an lightly staffed model from the FDD, so labor is adjusted down to 20–33% — the 40–55% category band assumes a staffed venue. Confirm actual staffing with Item 20 franchisees.

buildup.label: − Occupancy / rent (not disclosed)
buildup.note: Item 7 disclosed no rent figure — benchmark occupancy applied so the line above isn't overstated

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: lightly staffed · ≈ 2.4–4 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Education / childcare” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Read as lightly staffed from the FDD, which lowers the labor band applied below.
Item 7 disclosed no rent — occupancy estimated at the 10–18% category band.
Estimated at the 5–12% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 20–33% lightly staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: We couldn't extract enough of this franchisor's financial statements to assess its condition. Request the audited Item 21 financials and have them reviewed before committing.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. There is no parent guarantee of performance in this FDD, so Thinking Cup, Inc. is not contractually obligated to keep funding the franchisor or backing its commitments to you.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: no parent guarantee backing the franchisor's obligations to you
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
